title: cracca nubica
intro: Cracca nubica, also known as the Bearberry or Uva-ursi, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Europe, North America, and Asia, with small, white flowers and red berries. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of arbutin and ursolic acid. The health benefits of Cracca nubica include its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as burning urination and abdominal pain. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine to support kidney and urinary health.
